key_insight: An ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm can discover novel crystal structures without relying on existing structure databases, including the correct ground states for complex La-Co-Pb compounds.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster and more reliable inverse design of advanced quantum and magnetic materials by locating both stable and competing metastable phases in difficult energy landscapes.
method: ANN-based interatomic potential guides structure search
Iterative MMIGA adds metadynamics-inspired penalties to escape repeated minima
Search is applied to ternary La-Co-Pb systems with strong immiscibility and complexity
evidence_signal: The method correctly predicted the Pbam ground-state structure of La4Co4Pb and exactly matched the independently X-ray-determined orthorhombic structure of La5CoPb2.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the ANN potential and the ability of the iterative penalty scheme to explore sufficiently diverse minima in very complex landscapes.
paper_title: Complex crystal structure prediction using ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm
